entry.title: Re: How to update data in custom facet with the use of da...
entry.content:
Hi Dhaval,

Since you have created these fields in the standard xbo from sdk, you have to create odata extension scenario for standard xbo and add these custom field to desired standard odata. Post which the custom fields will start appearing in data workbench.

Please refer https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/new-feature-odata-service-extension-with-extension-fields-in-sdk/ba-p/13348038.

Thanks,

Jayant Saini

entry.title: HANA HotSpots General Reportの生成依頼
entry.content:
S4HANA DIGITS Initial Shipment Stack から S4HANA DIGITS SPS01 へのアップグレード後、様々なビジネストランザクション、特に更新系アクションで処理速度が低下する事象が発生しており、原因分析のためGeneral Reportをもとに調査を行いたいと考えております。

PERSON_NAME HotSpots General Reportを生成いただく、または生成方法をご教示いただくことは可能でしょうか。

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for having two company manager in one c...
entry.content:
Hi,

Unfortunately, it is not possible to have two managers per one org unit as the 'intercompany' approach is not supported in ByD so only one manager at the time can be assigned to an org unit.

Additionally, please find below the answers for more questions that may arise about the org unit assignments: 

1. Is it possible to have multiple managers for the same org unit on a different timeline?

Yes, it is possible to define a manager for an org unit based on a defined timeline.

For example, we want to have Manager1 for the org unit A1 for September and Manager2 for the same org unit A1 for October.

This can be done as following:

  1. Go to Organizational management work center
  2. Select Edit org structure
  3. Highlight the org unit you would like to edit, A1 in this case
  4. Enter the desired date for the 'Edit Organizational Structure - Effective From' in this case as follow:
    • For Manager1 - We have a date from today save the org structure, with today's date, the date will then appear from today to unlimited
    • For Manager2 - enter the date 01/10/2010 and save the org structure the date will then appear from today to unlimited

       

       2. Is it possible to have the same manager for multiple org unit?

      Yes, it is possible to have the same manager assigned to different org unit for different companies.

      For example, Employee1 is assigned as manager to org unit A1 from company A and org unit B1 from company B.

      3. Is it possible to have an employee assigned to the same org unit as employee and as manager of the org unit?

      Yes, it is possible to have the employee assigned as both manager and employee for the same org unit.

      For example, Employee1 is assigned to org unit A1 as employee and as manager of the org unit A1.

      Scenario:

      We have two companies A & B.

      Company A has org unit A1-A2-A3 assigned to it.

      Company B has org unit B1-B2-B3 assigned to it.

       

      For more details kindly refer to the KBA : 1505349

       

      Best regards,

      Darshan

entry.title: Re: HANA HotSpots General Reportの生成依頼

entry.content:

we will check this BAdi

Some User are not accepting any argument 😉 even if You say that milion of users works with standard MD04 they want....it different 😉

In fact this request is only some visual adjustments of the standard screen. In some places in SAP (like for example in Project System) SAP offer some more standardize SPRO features i was looking something similar for MD04 😉 

Anyway many THX

Pi 

 

Pi

entry.title: Stop workflow from triggering when vendor is changed using MASS transaction
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a requirement to change a specific field at BusinessPartner(Vendor) at company code level for a huge number of business partners. We thought of using 'MASS' transaction to do the changes and it is allowing us to do the changes as well. But, we noticed that it also triggers a workflow and we don't want to trigger the workflow for this action. This is a one time activity to change existing business partners. 

Can anyone share some idea on how to do this without triggering a workflow. Our last approach is to do via a custom program.

entry.title: Re: how to identify the highest number of rows and the largest size tables within a specific tablesp
entry.content: 6GB only? It's very small.

Thanks and regards,

Dhaval Solanki

+91 9898712915

entry.title: Orchestrate your Generative AI workflows through the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Authors: @amagnani |@jacobahtan |@thiago | @Trinidad

As we delve into the world of AI, it's essential to understand how orchestrating various components can enhance generative AI's efficiency and reliability. SAP's Generative AI Hub recently introduced a game-changing feature — Orchestration. This feature is designed to do more than just provide model access; it's about creating robust AI workflows that seamlessly integrate with your business applications.

This blog post is part of the webinar series Augment Your SAP BTP Use Cases With AI Foundation on SAP BTP exploring the Generative AI capabilities of SAP AI Foundation and presents proof of concepts through various use cases.

 

Understanding the Power of Orchestration

In the realm of generative AI, especially within a business context, simply connecting to a language model is often inadequate. Business applications require structured, context-rich, and reliable outputs to deliver value. This is where SAP’s Orchestration steps in, tying together various components like data pipelines, AI models, and prebuilt modules.

Orchestration is part of the Generative AI Hub and focuses on:

  • Connecting Components: Combines data pipelines, AI models, and modules like grounding and content filtering.
  • Reducing Maintenance: Simplifies the integration process, allowing businesses to focus on innovation.

Orchestration Key Features

Grounding

Grounding is crucial for contextual AI responses. It enriches the user's request with relevant business context, reducing the likelihood of AI hallucinations — false or irrelevant information generated by AI models. By automating document grounding, businesses can integrate relevant document indexing, updated daily, without manual intervention.

Manual grounding techniques involve chunking and embedding steps, offering granular control over the data process (check our previous blog Embedding Business Context with the SAP HANA Cloud, Vector Engine for more details). However, SAP’s Orchestration offers a streamlined alternative, connecting user queries to the business context through a retrieval layer. This involves:

  • Data Ingestion: Uploading relevant documents.
  • Preprocessing & Chunking: Breaking down documents into manageable parts.
  • Embedding & Similarity Search: Generating embeddings for efficient similarity search.

Prompt Templating

Prompt Templating enables the creation of templates containing placeholders that can be dynamically replaced with specific inputs or variables. This method facilitates the generation of diverse queries or instructions by substituting different values into the placeholders, thus simplifying the customization of prompts for various tasks or contexts. Prompt Templating produces the final query, which is then sent to the model.

Recently, a new Generative AI feature called Prompt Registry was introduced, providing a foundation for managing prompt templates throughout various AI scenarios. This feature supports the entire process, from design to runtime, and helps reduce the complexity associated with handling prompt templates. The Prompt Registry's functionalities can be accessed via APIs directly from the Generative AI Hub. Additionally, prompts stored in the Prompt Registry can be reused within the Orchestration module.

Data Masking

Despite the Generative AI Hub encrypting data by default, data masking is critical for additional privacy. It's about concealing sensitive information during processing and storage, as well as safeguarding against unauthorized access or bias.

The Data Masking capability offers a range of Masked Data Types you can select for masking, that are served by the SAP BTP Data Privacy Integration service APIs.

Two options are offered for Data Masking:

  • Anonymization: Replaces names with placeholders to protect personal data.
  • Pseudo-Anonymization: The orchestration service can reverse placeholders back to original text for final outputs.

Content Filtering

Content filtering safeguards content standards by offering both input and output filters. These filters ensure that AI responses align with your enterprise's guidelines, preventing inappropriate content.

  • Input Filtering: Checks incoming prompts for guideline alignment.
  • Output Filtering: Analyzes AI-generated responses to ensure they meet standards.

This dual-layer filtering enhances brand integrity, ensures regulatory compliance, and promotes user safety.

Harmonized API Management

The Orchestration feature also supports harmonized APIs, facilitating seamless model-switching by simply replacing the model name. This approach enhances developer efficiency by providing consistency across various generative AI models.

 

How to consume Orchestration capabilities

SAP AI Launchpad

The SAP AI Launchpad provides a low-code/no-code interface for configuring and accessing the orchestration features of the Generative AI Hub. It allows users to create and test orchestration workflows before integrating them into applications. The Launchpad includes a graphical interface as well as the option to generate workflows as JSON files.

Generative AI APIs and SDKs

For direct application integration, Generative AI orchestration capabilities are also accessible via APIs, eliminating the need for the SAP AI Launchpad.

SDKs

The Generative AI Hub offers a comprehensive suite of SDKs in various programming languages, designed to accelerate and simplify the development of custom AI solutions.

The SDKs provide an abstraction layer that reduces implementation complexity, automating tasks like OAuth token handling and deployment ID lookups, harmonizing LLM access, and facilitating orchestration capabilities such as content filtering and data masking. They support integration with frameworks like LangChain.
Three versions are available for you to choose:

  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI (JavaScript)  
  • Python SAP generative AI hub SDK
  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI (Java)

     

“Ask the City” Use Case

We would like to illustrate the Orchestration capabilities and how to implement them, with a practical use case that we have called “Ask the City”.

Business Scenario

In a previous blog Embedding Business Context with the SAP HANA Cloud, Vector Engine, we already explored the "Ask the City" use case, where our primary persona, Emma, a city resident, requested answers to her inquiries through a chat interface, eliminating the need to manually sift through the city's website pages. In our previous blog, we harnessed the power of SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine alongside Generative AI to implement retrieval-augmented generation, commonly referred to as grounding capabilities. This approach allowed us to maintain granular control over each phase of the process. We meticulously managed document chunking, leveraged GenAI LLMs for embedding generation, stored the resulting vectors within SAP HANA Cloud, and selected the most appropriate similarity search algorithm.

In today's blog, we'll explore the implementing the "Ask the City" use case using the grounding capability within the orchestration layer of the Generative AI Hub. By employing the orchestration module, we can significantly simplify our code, as it eliminates the need to manually handle the various steps involved in retrieval-augmented generation, or grounding. Additionally, this approach allows us to leverage other capabilities provided by the orchestration module, such as data masking and content filtering. These features enable us to extend our use case coverage, enhancing its functionality and efficiency.

Let’s demonstrate our prototype in action with a short demo video of the Ask The City chatbot.

Architecture

Let's now examine the architecture diagram of our prototype.

Citizens engage with the chatbot via a straightforward web chat embedded within the Sagenai City website.

This prototype

  • utilizes the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
  • is deployed on the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Runtime
  • implements generative AI capabilities through the Orchestration service of the Generative AI Hub, managing documents stored on a Microsoft SharePoint site and connecting to the configured foundation model.

Implementation Deep Dive

Let’s delve now into the detailed steps involved in implementing the orchestration capabilities of our prototype.

We've broken down this technical section into five steps for streamlined understanding:

  1. Generative AI Hub setup on our BTP subaccount
  2. SAP AI Core instance configuration to support grounding capabilities
  3. Orchestration configuration creation and deployment in SAP AI Core

For setting up our AI Core instance, we've utilized the AI Launchpad UI; however, you can alternatively use the corresponding APIs for configuration.

4. SharePoint repository setup, to store documents for grounding and integrating it with the grounding capabilities

 5. "Ask the City" prototype application implementation.

Source Code

Our Ask the City use case prototype source code is available in Github: 04-ask-city-orchestration-service

It includes:

  • cap-chatbot: Full-stack SAP Cloud Application Program Model(CAP) TypeScript implementation, integrated with Orchestration Service through @sap-ai-sdk/orchestration.
  • python-notebooks: Notebook demonstrating how to interact with Orchestration Service to implement the AskTheCity Use Case with orchestration service through SAP Generative AI Hub SDK.

 

Additional Resources for you to explore

To wrap things up, we've compiled a selection of links to orchestration documents and educational sessions that you might find beneficial:

  • Learning Journey Generative AI Hub
    • Using generative-AI-hub-SDK to interact with Orchestration Services (includes a Notebook example)
  • SAP Help Center
    • Orchestration Service in SAP AI Launchpad
    • Orchestration Service in SAP AI Core
    • Prompt Registry
  • TechEd sessions
    • AI201 | Development with our generative AI hub, SDKs, and orchestration features
    • AI106 - Harness retrieval-augmented generation in Joule and generative AI hub

Important Note: Conversion Rates for GenAI Tokens in Orchestration Modules supported regions for grounding

 

More sessions on this series

Don't miss out on our central blog post Augment Your SAP BTP Use Cases With AI Foundation on SAP BTP, where you'll find comprehensive details about the sessions in this series. This includes session recordings and related blog posts. Plus, you can easily register for upcoming sessions to continue expanding your knowledge and skills.

 

Conclusion and Takeaways

SAP’s Orchestration feature within the Generative AI Hub allowing businesses to harness AI's potential effectively. By providing document grounding, prompt templating, data masking, content filtering, and harmonized APIs, it empowers enterprises to focus on innovation rather than integration challenges. Orchestration capabilities ensure your AI solutions are not only smarter but also safer and more reliable, paving the way for advanced AI-driven business outcomes.

entry.title: not added to the banking hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi, all

I am trying to register a bank account in Fiori. When I attempt to save, the message "It is not added to the banking hierarchy" appears. When I check in GUI using FI12, the account is indeed not displayed. The bank itself is registered. How can I resolve this issue?

entry.title: Re: Working with HANA CV / MDS Cubes. Do not see any addi...
entry.content: While you can select individual private attributes, you can only select complete shared dimensions (the names of each of the joined dimensional CV). This means: By selecting the names of the dimensional CVs you automatically include all of their attributes.

entry.title: Re: none Evaluate Capacity(F6798) in SAP S4HANA Public cl...
entry.content:
Hi @SI_y_Ito,

You are correct that the application cannot be found using keywords. I have found a way to help locate the application. Here is the documentation I referred to: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003315026

To start:

1. Go to the homepage of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
2. Click on Account.
3. Select App Finder, as shown in the image below:

Next, search for the Evaluate Capacity application in App Finder.
Once you find it, open the application. The image below shows how it looks:

The result of the application’s work will be shown below:

Here is the documentation that will help create a scenario for Evaluate Capacity:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/streamlining-capacity-evaluation-with-the-sap-fiori-app/ba-p/13926624

Additionally, here is the documentation for the application itself:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/21aead0c98bd4755abdacd91c99e3393/0e8e338735514e4c89b5e45f180c1c1e.html?locale=en-US&version=2023.000

Please note that according to the documentation, you need to assign the SAP_BR_PRODN_PLNR role to work with this application.

BR,
Andrei

entry.title: Re: Updating Short text of Inspection Lot using BO Interf...
entry.content:
Hi @Jose3,

I reviewed the documentation and repeated the scenario related to updating the short text of the inspection lot. Here’s what I came up with based on the code from the documentation, and everything worked fine for me. Please try using this code:

DATA(lv_inspectionlot) = '010000000000'.
DATA(lv_inspectionlottext) = 'new value'.

MODIFY ENTITIES OF I_InspectionLotTP_2
ENTITY inspectionlot
UPDATE FIELDS ( inspectionlottext )
WITH VALUE #( ( InspectionLot = lv_inspectionlot
InspectionLotText = lv_inspectionlottext ) )

MAPPED DATA(ls_mapped)
FAILED DATA(ls_failed)
REPORTED DATA(ls_reported).

IF ls_failed IS INITIAL.

COMMIT ENTITIES RESPONSE OF I_InspectionLotTP_2
FAILED DATA(failed)
REPORTED DATA(reported).

ENDIF.

To verify the functionality of this code, I created a lot and, as shown in the screenshots below, the text and inspection lot were updated:

After executing the code, the short text was updated as expected, and the result is shown below:

I hope this helps you resolve the issue.

BR,
Andrei

entry.title: Can't choose Cloud Foundry when create Project in BAS
entry.content:
When I create new Project BAS, if I choose Data source = Connect to a System, can't choose Cloud Foundry in deployment configuration.

As I created before I can choose Cloud Foundry.

Could you please help me to solve this issue?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Re: Sending purchase orders by type
entry.content: Yes, just create an output condition with the document type. It will not be an exception. Just use it to maintain records for document types you want to send, and not for those that should not be sent out to the suppliers. There is many tutorials how to do that.

entry.title: Re: RAP Application
entry.content: Just literally, in this file you are define your BO`s behaviors during the transactional process, e.g. locking, authorization, CRUD....detail please check https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/rap-bo-behavior?version=s4hana_cloud

entry.title: Re: How to Pre-Filter data in table using Fiori Elements ?
entry.content: Please look at the sample provided in the blog. I wonder if this can be explained better than in the blog. Here is are two clues: The first view is automatically selected. You may provide only one view.

entry.title: Re: Unable to consume data from a odata service
entry.content:
See this blog by one of our esteemed community members 😉: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/handling-images-in-sap-build-apps/ba-p/13558666

For base64, at least in he image component, you can set the source to the base64 string, but you must add the prefix: 

data:image/png;base64,

I am assuming his is the issue, but if it is something else, let me know.

entry.title: Navigating SAP Customizations: A Step-by-Step approach to a Clean(er) Core with S/4HANA
entry.content:
When I was a kid, I read a note at my doctor’s clinic:

“On one side, my livelihood depends on people’s suffering, but on the other, I am grateful that I’ve been given the opportunity to relieve their pain and help them feel better.”

That sentiment has always stayed with me—especially now that I am a SAP consultant. While I benefit professionally from helping companies that struggle with complex SAP solutions, I am driven by a genuine desire to ease those struggles, maximize the return on their SAP investments, and deliver real business value. In other words, I want to help “heal” their pain points.

In this post, I am sharing a framework for customizing SAP without overcomplicating the system—a way to minimize the “suffering” that stems from excessive modifications while still delivering the specific functionalities your organization needs.

1. Apply a Zero-Modification Policy

 

  • What It Means: Refrain from modifying standard SAP code. SAP S/4HANA and other SAP solutions come with well-established best practices, so start by leveraging standard features to the fullest extent.
  • Key Benefit: Avoiding direct changes to standard code reduces the risk of upgrade and patch conflicts. With fewer dependencies, you can adopt new SAP functionalities more quickly and smoothly.

 

2. Evaluate If Key-User Extensibility Fits

 

  • How It Works: SAP offers key-user tools (called “In-App Extensibility”) that let power users modify fields, adapt forms, and tweak the UI—all without deep coding.
  • Why It Matters: This approach is faster and less invasive. Because it lives within the SAP-recommended extensibility framework, updates and patches remain simpler.

 

Key Metrics to Track

 

  • Time-to-Implement: Measure how quickly you can deploy changes using key-user tools versus classical ABAP developments.
  • User Adoption Rate: Assess whether key users find these tools intuitive and how much IT intervention is needed.

 

3. Identify Where Customizations Are Truly Needed

 

  • What This Step Means: If key-user tools don’t fulfill your requirements, consider a more advanced approach—but verify that deeper customization is genuinely necessary.
  • Guiding Questions:

 

Can an existing process be adjusted or reconfigured to avoid new custom code?

Does a standard SAP or third-party solution address your needs?

Common Pitfall

“Customization Creep”: Over-engineering even minor requirements can pile on technical debt. Always check if SAP’s out-of-the-box capabilities—or minor configuration adjustments—can solve the problem first.

4. Analyze If the Customization Is Necessary

 

  • Why This Matters: Identifying a gap doesn’t always mean you should fill it with custom code. This step evaluates whether the benefits truly outweigh the costs. Even a legitimate need can become a burden if the maintenance, testing, and upgrade overhead outweigh the functionality gained.
  • Evaluation Factors:

 

Business Value: Will new features measurably enhance productivity, compliance, or revenue?

Long-Term Impact: How likely is it that future SAP updates could break this customization? Is the maintenance effort justifiable over time?

Conduct a Cost-Benefit Analysis. Factor in both development and maintenance costs, then compare them to the potential ROI—be it time savings, reduced errors, or improved profitability. This ensures you only invest in customizations that deliver clear, long-term value.

5. Embrace Side-by-Side Extensibility

 

  • What It Is: Instead of embedding code directly into the SAP core, build extensions on external platforms such as SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
  • Advantages:

 

Isolation: Keeps the core ERP stable by separating custom logic.

Scalability: Cloud-based apps can adapt as your business grows or evolves.

Reusability: APIs and microservices can be repurposed across multiple SAP and non-SAP systems.

6. Resort to Classical ABAP Customization When Necessary

 

  • When to Use It: For highly specialized or performance-critical needs that aren’t met by in-app or side-by-side solutions.
  • Best Practices:

 

Deviation Tracker: Keep a record of what changed, why, and who approved it.

Use Enhancement Points: SAP-provided “BAdIs” (Business Add-Ins) or enhancement frameworks reduce the risk of breaking standard code.

Governance & Testing: Maintain strict code reviews and regular regression tests to avoid introducing systemic issues.

Periodically review your ABAP custom objects. Some might be replaced by new standard features in the latest SAP release, helping you reduce technical debt.

Clean Core Challenges

1. Cultural Mindset

 

  • Challenge: A longstanding belief that customization is the only way to meet exact requirements.
  • Solution: Promote an ROI-based decision-making process, showing that fewer modifications often speed up upgrades and reduce costs.

 

2. Legacy Custom Code

 

  • Challenge: Older systems may already have extensive, mission-critical customizations.
  • Solution: Identify and gradually retire or refactor outdated code. New SAP features may now serve the same purpose more elegantly.

 

3. Complex Business Requirements

 

  • Challenge: Some processes—especially in regulated industries—exceed what standard tools can offer.
  • Solution: Use side-by-side extensibility or developer extensibility judiciously, ensuring robust design practices and strong governance.

 

4. Limitations of Clean Core APIs and BAdIs

 

  • Challenge: Modern APIs and BAdIs may lack the depth and flexibility of classic equivalents, limiting their ability to address complex requirements.
  • Solution: Analyze gaps early in the design phase and leverage side-by-side extensions or custom APIs to address unmet needs. Monitor SAP's roadmap for upcoming enhancements.

 

5. Governance & Policy Adherence

 

  • Challenge: Under tight deadlines, teams might sidestep zero-modification policies for quick fixes.
  • Solution: Create a governance board and standardized templates for cost-benefit analyses to maintain consistency.

 

6. Perceived Limitations of Standard

 

  • Challenge: Users might assume SAP’s standard capabilities are too limited.
  • Solution: Provide demos or proofs of concept to show how versatile SAP actually is. Accelerate your integrations, extensions, and innovations with SAP Business Accelerator Hub

 

7. Balancing Innovation and Stability

 

  • Challenge: Teams want rapid innovation without risking core stability.
  • Solution: Keep your core as untouched as possible and innovate externally—for instance, using agile development on SAP BTP.

 

Measuring Success

 

  1. Customization Density The ratio of custom objects to standard objects—lower is typically better for smooth upgrades, since fewer custom dependencies mean fewer points of conflict.
  2. Upgrade Cycle Efficiency The time needed to adapt custom code during new releases or patches.
  3. Incidents Tracked How many production issues are tied to custom code vs. standard functionality.

 

Legacy Modernization Strategy

For organizations dealing with large swaths of legacy code, map each customization’s usage and importance. Often, newer SAP enhancements or frameworks replicate or surpass older custom logic. Prioritize retiring or refactoring the highest-cost, least-used customizations first for immediate gains in stability and maintenance efficiency.

Much like the doctor’s note that inspired me as a child—balancing the inevitability of pain with the privilege of providing relief—SAP customization should aim to resolve genuine “pain points” without creating new ones. By embracing a zero-modification mindset, leveraging key-user and side-by-side extensibility, and judiciously using classical ABAP only where absolutely necessary, you’ll keep your SAP environment healthy, upgrade-friendly, and truly valuable.

What’s your experience?How does your organization approach SAP customization? Share your insights or challenges in the comments or reach out directly—I would love to learn from your journey and collaborate on reducing complexity while maximizing SAP’s full potential.

 

SAP S/4HANA SAP Build SAP BTP, ABAP environment

entry.title: Re: Restrict the non monthly components or Standard Process - EC to ECP data replication
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Possible to use different weights/ratings in PM Form...
entry.content: Hi Mridul, thanks for the confirmation! That's what I've thought... what also maybe is a possibility is to make Section Weight editable on PM Forms... In this way, some roles have the possibility to adap the Section Weight per User Type?

entry.title: Re: Restrict the non monthly components or Standard Process - EC to ECP data replication
entry.content: Hi, were you able to resolve it? EX_PAOCF_EC_EXCLUDE_FROM_DELET BADI is only to avoid deletion of manually maintained WTs in ECP.So if a particular WT in Pay component Recurring (Compensation) needs to be excluded from IT 0008 replication, how can it be done?

entry.title: Re: Best Practices to Deploy MDK Application in Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
@bill_froelichthe use of the App is only on Android and not in the Web Version then I deploy in Mobile Service. (I also deployed at the beginning in Cloud Foundry for test app purpose).

When I deploy the app in Mobile Service, BAS ask me to log into Cloud Foundry and select a Space.

entry.title: Re: SAP TM Private Cloud Implementation - Query
entry.content:
Hello @Saurabh_Mittal 

Side-by-side TM deployment integrates with ERP with External TM System Integration

As the customer already runs on premise S/4HANA, they can also consider implementing embedded TM right in their existing S/4HANA installation. The integration is then done with Internal TM Component Integration

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP TM Private Cloud Implementation - Query
entry.content: bgRFC is a technique used by TM to implement deferred processing. As such bgRFC is not really an integration functionality. bgRFC is used both in embedded and side-by-side TM deployments.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam - CAP/HANA in Berlin
entry.content:
We have a CodeJam scheduled with Zalando in Berlin Germany on January, the 17th 2025.

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/combine-sap-cloud-application-programming-model-with-sap-hana-cloud-to/ev-p/13977610

There are still some seats left open, if you want to join myself and @Vitaliy-R for a nice day coding and learning click the link above!

Can't wait to see you in Berlin!

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - Delete Item Persistent Storage
entry.content:
An ID is generated for every record and you must use this ID to identify the record you want to delete.

For example, I have a UI that lets me create a record with a random UUID, and a delete button to delete that particular record.

I have an on-device storage entity with 2 fields plus the default ID field.

The set logic

The Create Record is just setting the name field to a random UUID.

The delete logic 

The Delete Record only has data resource and ID fields.

And for the ID I select Data Item in Repeat and select the ID field.

P.S.: Strangely, it is permitted to delete the ID field. Then it seems you could not select a specific row but you'd have select a collection and then add your own logic to find the record you want.

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 10.0 SQL - DTW Authentication Problem
entry.content:
Hi IcaroParra,

If you maximize the 'Authenticating' window, do you see anything?

Can you try to remove/delete the WebView2 cache folder from C:\ProgramData\SAP\WebView2 CS and try to login into DTW again?

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: How to use SAP Content Agent Service (Video)
entry.content:
Thanks @BorisZarske !

It is working now.

Best regards,

Sumit

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch am 17.02.2025
entry.content:
Der nächste SAP Stammtisch Magdeburg steht bevor!

Alles wird sich ums Thema "Zukunftstends" drehen, wenn wir uns abweichend vom üblichen Datum (auf Grund von Ferien) am 17. Februar 2025 im UCC Magdeburg treffen. Eine digitale Teilnahme per Zoom wird ebenfalls ermöglicht.

Agenda
Bericht der Barcamp Ergebnisse und Eindrücke (https://www.linkedin.com/events/barcampelbfabrik7270748242378735616/)
weitere Themen tba

Schaut vorbei, wir freuen uns stets über neue Gesichter aus der Magdeburger (SAP- und Non-SAP)-Community! Zur besseren Planung bitten wir bei Teilnahme um eine Zusage im Event.

____________

The next SAP Stammtisch Magdeburg is coming up!

Everything will revolve around the topic of “Future Trends” when we meet at the UCC Magdeburg on February 17, 2025, in deviation from the usual date (due to vacations). Digital participation via Zoom will also be possible.

Agenda
Report of the Barcamp results and impressions (https://www.linkedin.com/events/barcampelbfabrik7270748242378735616/)
Further topics tba

Drop by, we are always happy to see new faces from the Magdeburg (SAP and non-SAP) community! For better planning, please confirm your participation in the event.

entry.title: Is it possible to change the text of standard message displayed when we click on Close during Moves
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is it possible to change the text of standard message displayed when we click on Close button in the Move In/Move out process in SAP Service Cloud v2.

Steps to reproduce:

1. Login into SAP Service Cloud v2.

2. Open the Premise in the detailed view.

3. Click on Create button >> Select Move In.

4. Click on Close button.

5. Pop is displayed with 'Are you sure you want to cancel the process?' message.

Is it possible to edit the text of below highlighted message in SAP Service Cloud v2. 

If Possible, kindly help me with the steps to customize pop up text.

 

Regards,

Syed Muheed

entry.title: Re: RAP factory action: Response with deep result possible?
entry.content:
According to SAP support, this is not possible. The proposed workaround is to take the root BO and request the children by association.

A solution that I came up with is to use a custom handler as Andre Fischer described in a blog post.

There I call the standard handler extract the keys of my root BO from the result, read the children using EML and create the response.

entry.title: Re: How to add Snowflake as a source for SAP Datasphere or SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @RajeshG1227 , just to clarify, in our adapter we do not explicitly invoke the snowflake commands show columns in account and show primarykeys in account. So this is generated by the snowflake jdbc driver itself apparently. What we use are standard JDBC calls, such as DatabaseMetaData.getColumns and DatabaseMetaData.getPrimaryKeys and these filter based on the database name, schema name ...

If you read the snowflake documentation https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/show-columns and https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/show-primary-keys it is possible to describe the columns and primary keys of a table in a given database and schema.

So just to doublecheck, what is the URL you are constructing to connect to snowflake from datasphere?

Can you post this or at least anonymize the sensitive parts of it?

entry.title: Subcontracting of equipment through maintenance order
entry.content: We have multiple pieces of equipment for which we are performing third-party refurbishment under maintenance orders. However, this functionality is not available in SAP Public Cloud. Could you please provide a workaround for internal and external refurbishment/sub-contracting through maintenance orders?

entry.title: Re: SLT Replication will start Without Initial Load ?
entry.content:
Please see

https://help.sap.com/doc/6a30dd8845174864a0ee77c21f2fcbbf/2.0.20/en-US/Resuming_Replication.pdf

Regards.

David

entry.title: Do we have such role which can grant read only permission for SAP PO Integration Builder(ID) access
entry.content: We have a requirement to allow read only access to a user in the PO Integration Builder(ID). Can we achieve this?

entry.title: Barcamp in der Elbfabrik Magdeburg
entry.content:
Produktion, Logistik, Energie und Mensch im Kontext von Datenräumen, KI und Automatisierung

Lust auf spannende Diskussionen und neue Fachkontakte? Dann sind Sie bei unserem Barcamp in der Elbfabrik des Fraunhofer IFF genau richtig! Wir laden herzlich ein, dabei zu sein und gemeinsam mit uns die Zukunft der Produktion zu gestalten.

Was erwartet Sie?
Netzwerken: Lernen Sie andere Expert:innen und Gleichgesinnte kennen.
Aktuelle Themen: Diskutieren Sie über die neuesten Trends und Entwicklungen.
Innovationen: Entwickeln Sie gemeinsam kreative Lösungen.
Erfahrungen teilen: Tauschen Sie sich über Ihre Erfahrungen und Ideen aus.

Wie läuft das Barcamp ab?
Unser Barcamp folgt dem „Barcamp light“-Ansatz. Das bedeutet, Sie können eigene Themen und Herausforderungen im Kontext der Vernetzung und Effizienzsteigerung der Produktion einbringen und zur Diskussion stellen. Natürlich haben wir auch ein paar spannende Themen vorbereitet, damit es nie langweilig wird.
Zu Beginn stellen wir die Themen vor und Sie können per Abstimmung entscheiden, welche diskutiert werden. In den Sessions geht es dann ans Eingemachte: Wir erarbeiten gemeinsam Lösungen und tauschen uns intensiv aus. Jede Session wird von einer moderierenden Person begleitet, damit alles reibungslos läuft.
Am Ende präsentieren wir die Ergebnisse der Diskussionen. Das kann entweder im Plenum oder in einer „Vernissage“ geschehen. Zur Dokumentation der Ergebnisse stellen wir eine Vorlage zur Verfügung.

Wer ist dabei?
Technologie-Anbieter (zum Beispiel in4MD Service GmbH, BA Business Advice GmbH, Fraunhofer IFF, Mittelstand-Digital Zentrum Magdeburg, SAP UCC Magdeburg)
Unternehmen und externe Teilnehmer:innen (zum Beispiel Fachleute aus Produktionsleitung, Instandhaltung etc.)
Technologieinteressierte Expert:innen aus der Region

Wann und Wo?
Das Barcamp findet am 6.2.2025 im Technikum, den Denkzellen und im Open-Space-Bereich der Elbfabrik des Fraunhofer IFF statt.

Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Teilnahme und darauf, gemeinsam mit Ihnen die Zukunft der Produktion zu gestalten!

__________

Production, logistics, energy and people in the context of data spaces, AI and automation

Fancy exciting discussions and new professional contacts? Then our Barcamp at the Fraunhofer IFF's Elbfabrik is the right place for you! We cordially invite you to join us and shape the future of production together with us.

What can you expect?
Networking: Get to know other experts and like-minded people.
Current topics: Discuss the latest trends and developments.
Innovations: Develop creative solutions together.
Share experiences: Exchange your experiences and ideas.

How does the Barcamp work?
Our Barcamp follows the “Barcamp light” approach. This means that you can bring in your own topics and challenges in the context of networking and increasing efficiency in production and put them up for discussion. Of course, we have also prepared a few exciting topics so that it never gets boring.
At the beginning, we will present the topics and you can decide which ones to discuss by voting. The sessions then get down to business: We work out solutions together and exchange ideas intensively. Each session is accompanied by a moderator to ensure that everything runs smoothly.
At the end, we present the results of the discussions. This can take place either in a plenary session or in a “vernissage”. We provide a template to document the results.

Who is involved?
Technology providers (e.g. BA Business Advice GmbH, Fraunhofer IFF, Mittelstand-Digital Zentrum Magdeburg, SAP UCC)
Companies and external participants (e.g. experts from production management, maintenance, etc.)
Experts from the region interested in technology

When and where?
The barcamp will take place on February 6, 2025 in the Fraunhofer IFF's technical center, think tanks and open space area of the Elbfabrik.

We look forward to your participation and to shaping the future of production together with you!

entry.title: Re: Functional Usage - Value Drivers and Peer Group Comparison
entry.content:
@UlfZeisberger

I understand your frustration, but to avoid even more frustration I would hardly recommend you
to use the right channels and the right contact(s) to address and discuss your requests.
Unfortunately, I cannot help you with new requirements regarding SAP for Me notifications
as we are responsible for the reporting related topics in SAP for Me. 
If you have any suggestions to the Customer Insights reporting or to this particular new section
for Functional Usage, please address it to us, your input is very welcome at this place.

entry.title: FB60 - Postings with Captive and Pro-rata VAT
entry.content:
Dear experts,

How can I post a vendor invoice through FB60 with both Captive and Pro-rata VAT?

Could you show prints of a posting including the aforementioned requirements?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Fiori Rich Text Editor - content visible only when Edit is clicked.
entry.content:
It is not possible to see content in standard mode for “Overview”, whereas when “Edit” is clicked then we can see content.

Rich Text Area is blank, in Standard mode: 

Rich Text Area content is visible, in Edit mode:

Any help would be gratefully appreciated, thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Tax code for sales and purchase
entry.content:
Consider adding a tag such as Goods and Services Tax (GST India) or a relevant localization one for your system as your problem seems related to India localization, and TAXINN doesn't handle classical codes as MWS and VST

  • Configuration of Tax Calculation Procedure TAXINN

entry.title: Prerequisites and Post steps/checks for Solman SPS upgrade
entry.content:
Dear All,

My Solution Manager  is 7.2 system, currently running on SPS 17 and HANA DB and it is critical to our environment.

I am planning to upgrade the system from SPS 17 to the latest SPS available in the marketplace. The system is configured with CHARM, TREX, Managed System Configuration, and SOS setup for all connected systems.

For example:

Should the CHARM setup be disabled or enabled before starting the SPS upgrade (prerequisite)?
Do we need to re-run every step in Mandatory Configuration after completing the SPS upgrade (post-upgrade)?

Could you please provide a detailed list of prerequisites and post-upgrade steps to be followed for both the Solman ABAP and JAVA stacks during this upgrade?

Regards
Appuru

entry.title: Re: Service Consumption Model 2 for OData Client Proxy
entry.content:
@shahbhat 
Support of two namespaces out of the box is still open. 😞

entry.title: Re: Service Consumption Model 2 for OData Client Proxy
entry.content:
@vkesarir 
Regards your 404 error: " 'The remote service has raised a Client Error '404': 'Not Found'"

I would recommend to use the cross trace to identify how the URL looks like.

entry.title: 投资项目向下传递结算规则功能
entry.content: 投资项目向下传递结算规则功能 我们想在此处简单说明在S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition的EPPM模块中投资项目的向下传递结算规则功能。本文档按照标准业务流程设计说明此功能，此功能包含以下两个参数： 结算规则维护：含向下传递选项（Settlement Rule Maintenance：With Pass-Down Option） 向下传递结算规则：是（Pass-Down Settlement Rule：YES） 要点提示： 选择“含向下传递选项”且勾选“向下传递结算规则”的投资项目不支持金额结算。 选择“含向下传递选项”且勾选“向下传递结算规则”的投资项目，其下阶WBS要素如果勾选了“向下传递结算规则”复选框，则当前WBS要素在系统层面定义为独立结算要素，支持独立维护结算规则；如果下阶WBS要素没有勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框，则当前WBS要素的结算依赖于项目（Project Definition），不支持独立维护结算规则，需使用系统应用“生成结算规则 – 项目”自动生成结算规则，其结算规则继承项目（Project Definition）的结算规则。 投资项目仅在释放项目之前“结算规则维护“和“向下传递结算规则”字段支持更改。投资项目释放后或者初步结算后（项目结算至AuC），“向下传递结算规则”复选框在编辑状态下显示可以更改，但在保存时系统会显示报错，提示当前项目状态不允许更改此参数。所以在设定向下传递结算规则参数时需保持谨慎。 新建投资项目 在投资项目（Project Definition）的初始创建界面可以在“结算规则维护“下拉列表中选择“含向下传递选项”或“不含向下传递选项”。如果选择“含向下传递选项”，则“向下传递结算规则”复选框将可供选择。如果在创建项目抬头时选中“向下传递结算规则”复选框，则结算规则将向下传递到其子 WBS 要素。 如果在创建投资项目（Project Definition）的初始界面没有选择“含向下传递选项”以及勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框，则在创建下阶WBS 要素时无法选择向下传递结算规则选项。 仅在释放项目之前“结算规则维护“和“向下传递结算规则”字段支持更改。 释放投资项目 在整体释放投资项目之后，系统会基于投资项目中的每一个WBS要素自动创建匹配的投资计量在建工程。 记录项目成本 执行初步项目结算 投资项目的初步结算是将成本从WBS要素结算至每一个WBS要素自动生成的投资计量在建工程，用户无需维护结算规则，由系统自动生成对应的结算规则。 在运行完成投资项目的初步结算之后检查自动生成的结算规则（结算类型AuC）可以发现，所有WBS要素的成本都结算至对应自动生成的投资计量在建工程中。 维护最终结算规则 手工维护结算规则 系统结果显示选择“含向下传递选项”且勾选“向下传递结算规则”的投资项目不支持金额结算，仅支持百分比结算和权数结算。 以TS001.1为例，该WBS要素勾选了“向下传递结算规则”复选框，所以TS001.1在系统层面定义为独立结算要素，支持独立维护结算规则。 而TS001.1的下阶WBS要素TS001.1.1，该WBS要素没有勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框，所以TS001.1.1的结算依赖于项目（Project Definition）, 结算规则的维护界面会显示不可编辑。 自动生成结算规则 自动生成结算规则程序运行的结果显示，勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框的WBS要素定义为独立结算要素，无需继承结算规则；而没有勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框的WBS要素，其结算规则需以继承的方式生成。 检查具体生成的结算规则可以发现除了TS001.1作为独立结算要素以外，其他所有下阶WBS要素的结算规则都继承于项目（Project Definition）。

entry.title: Set up maximum limit for stock level
entry.content:
Hi,

My requirements is to setup maxim stock limit for purchased materials. For example:

Max stock limit is 1000 PC

Demand is 1500 PC.

Expected MRP run result - purchase requisitions are created only for 1000 PC. Is there any valid solution?

Please notice, that using combination of fields "Maximum stock level" + "Lot Sizing Procedure = HB" does not cover the requirement. Because we do not need replenishment to max. level. We needed just limit, over which goods can not be ordered during MRP run.

Thanks.

entry.title: Query OQUT ORDR
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I'm looking for a query that returns the offers that have been closed but have no order. i.e. they have been closed manually.

The query I'm using is not working and is returning all closed offers, no matter if there is an order or not. I just want those that have no offer. 

Can anyone help me? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP Best Practice for Implicit Enhancements
entry.content:
This must be part of Clean Core, you may learn this concept from here: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/practicing-clean-core-extensibility-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud/introducing-the-clean-core-approach_fcb6c662-7041-4c99-88bd-345636fae7f3

The implicit enhancement during the system upgrade will probably break the SAP`s new feature. As it is unknown to SAP. So, it is better to use the BAdI.

entry.title: 投资项目向下传递结算规则功能
entry.content: 投资项目向下传递结算规则功能 我们想在此处简单说明在S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition的EPPM模块中投资项目的向下传递结算规则功能。本文档按照标准业务流程设计说明此功能，此功能包含以下两个参数： • 结算规则维护：含向下传递选项（Settlement Rule Maintenance：With Pass-Down Option） • 向下传递结算规则：是（Pass-Down Settlement Rule：YES） 要点提示： • 选择“含向下传递选项”且勾选“向下传递结算规则”的投资项目不支持金额结算。 • 选择“含向下传递选项”且勾选“向下传递结算规则”的投资项目，其下阶WBS要素如果勾选了“向下传递结算规则”复选框，则当前WBS要素在系统层面定义为独立结算要素，支持独立维护结算规则；如果下阶WBS要素没有勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框，则当前WBS要素的结算依赖于项目（Project Definition），不支持独立维护结算规则，需使用系统应用“生成结算规则 – 项目”自动生成结算规则，其结算规则继承项目（Project Definition）的结算规则。 • 投资项目仅在释放项目之前“结算规则维护“和“向下传递结算规则”字段支持更改。投资项目释放后或者初步结算后（项目结算至AuC），“向下传递结算规则”复选框在编辑状态下显示可以更改，但在保存时系统会显示报错，提示当前项目状态不允许更改此参数。所以在设定向下传递结算规则参数时需保持谨慎。 1. 新建投资项目 在投资项目（Project Definition）的初始创建界面可以在“结算规则维护“下拉列表中选择“含向下传递选项”或“不含向下传递选项”。如果选择“含向下传递选项”，则“向下传递结算规则”复选框将可供选择。如果在创建项目抬头时选中“向下传递结算规则”复选框，则结算规则将向下传递到其子 WBS 要素。 如果在创建投资项目（Project Definition）的初始界面没有选择“含向下传递选项”以及勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框，则在创建下阶WBS 要素时无法选择向下传递结算规则选项。 仅在释放项目之前“结算规则维护“和“向下传递结算规则”字段支持更改。 2. 释放投资项目 在整体释放投资项目之后，系统会基于投资项目中的每一个WBS要素自动创建匹配的投资计量在建工程。 3. 记录项目成本 4. 执行初步项目结算 投资项目的初步结算是将成本从WBS要素结算至每一个WBS要素自动生成的投资计量在建工程，用户无需维护结算规则，由系统自动生成对应的结算规则。 在运行完成投资项目的初步结算之后检查自动生成的结算规则（结算类型AuC）可以发现，所有WBS要素的成本都结算至对应自动生成的投资计量在建工程中。 5. 维护最终结算规则 1) 手工维护结算规则 系统结果显示选择“含向下传递选项”且勾选“向下传递结算规则”的投资项目不支持金额结算，仅支持百分比结算和权数结算。 以TS001.1为例，该WBS要素勾选了“向下传递结算规则”复选框，所以TS001.1在系统层面定义为独立结算要素，支持独立维护结算规则。 而TS001.1的下阶WBS要素TS001.1.1，该WBS要素没有勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框，所以TS001.1.1的结算依赖于项目（Project Definition）, 结算规则的维护界面会显示不可编辑。 2) 自动生成结算规则 自动生成结算规则程序运行的结果显示，勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框的WBS要素定义为独立结算要素，无需继承结算规则；而没有勾选“向下传递结算规则”复选框的WBS要素，其结算规则需以继承的方式生成。 检查具体生成的结算规则可以发现除了TS001.1作为独立结算要素以外，其他所有下阶WBS要素的结算规则都继承于项目（Project Definition）。

entry.title: Re: What you need to get and stay certified
entry.content:
Hello,

to do the certification, can I just buy the certification attempts (for example CER006) as before and then schedule the exam?

Or do I have to subscripe the SAP learning hub, is that mandatory to do the certification?

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: House bank configuration
entry.content: Sensitive fields (such as bank accounts) trigger a validation request in both create and modify mode, making FK08 mandatory (and in best practice, the creator/modifier should not be allowed to validate his own actions).

entry.title: Re: Is it Possible to create a column in Variable Pay tha...
entry.content: No unfortunately that is not possible. @juanantonioga

entry.title: Re: Stock Transfer - In-Plant (SAP What's New Release)
entry.content:
Hello Oksana,

First and foremost, the "What’s New" section is correct!
Regarding your question, the stock type "Restricted-use stock" generally refers to the use of batches. In other words, if you don't use "restricted" batches as a stock type, no app can display this type of stock.
For more information, see the SAP Help Portal: Batch Status Management.

Regards,
Carsten

entry.title: Discover Industries Learning Pages on SAP Learning
entry.content:
Discover #Learning Pages for ##Industries on SAP Learning (learning.sap.com)

We are excited to announce the launch of our new learning pages for various industries on SAP Learning! Whether you are a beginner or an experienced professional, our new industry-specific learning pages are designed to help you gain the skills and knowledge you need to excel in your field.

Explore our comprehensive learning resources tailored to industries such as:

  • Agribusiness: Manage operations productively and cost-effectively
  • Automotive: Manage operations productively and cost-effectively
  • Consumer Products: Integrate sustainability for long-term success
  • Life Sciences: Drive digital transformation to be future-ready
  • Public Sector: Deliver better public value
  • Professional Services: Manage services more effectively and reduce risk
  • Retail: Create shopping experiences that delight customers
  • Sports and Entertainment: Manage performance and improve the fan experience
  • Utilities: Embrace the green energy transformation

more learning pages for further industries are coming soon. 

Each page offers a curated selection of courses, tutorials, and certifications that are relevant to your industry. Our goal is to provide you with the most up-to-date and practical learning materials to support your career growth and development.

Most of the training content is free for internal SAP colleagues, partners, and customers, making it easier for you to access the resources you need. Additionally, each course comes with a Record of Achievement badge if you answer the assessment questions positively.

Thanks, 

Greg

Grzegorz Posyniak
Global Portfolio Manager for Industries,
SAP Product & Solution Learning, SIX Solution and Innovation Experience

entry.title: Re: (Re)using Master Operations in SAP Digital Manufacturing

That's interesting, great blog mate! I'm just wondering how to achieve the similar with Recipes for Process Industries?

Any thoughts?

Thanks.

Cheers,
Bruno

Hello SAP experts,

is there any possibility to do mass changes in outbound deliveries in S/4HANA Public Cloud?

We tried find something useful but we did not find anything.

Thank you

D.

#publiccloud

entry.title: Re: VL10B : Error V50R024 when i create a new delivery af...
entry.content:
We experienced the same thing, and found that the STO was open in another window. so system given this error, please check the STO will not remain in change mode.

for further you may use SM12.

entry.title: Re: Video Data Story, a brand-new way of story telling in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
It is a pity that in the further development of the SAC, such fancy functions are being rushed into, while basic functionalities are still not good. I'll just mention the table widget as an example.

We really didn't wait for the Video Data Story and we don't need it in the company. Sometimes I wish the responsible product managers at SAP would sit in a company for a week and see what challenges SAC users are struggling with and how things are really done.

Could you Help me?

Thanks,

@PhilipKoch 

Regarding your question "Pascal case vs. underscore case".See the "Model class" part of the article, number 3. In your define method you will find the EDM name.

entry.title: Re: Service Consumption Model 2 for OData Client Proxy

@FelipeRodriguez-018h 

service definition is SCM 1. You need at least release 2311 for the Model class.

entry.title: Re: Issues with MDK Client Version 2405 Compared to App S...
entry.content:
Hi, 

the last MDK client did crash - but please use the latest MDK client, this does not crash in my case and you should be able to solve that issue. I have tested both iOS and android and now it works fine.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: Service Consumption Model 2 for OData Client Proxy
entry.content:
@Balu483 

regenerate is an open point. You have to create a new one and delete the old.

entry.title: Re: none Evaluate Capacity(F6798) in SAP S4HANA Public cl...
entry.content: 同じ質問が2回投稿されたので、閉じます。

entry.title: How to View deleted delivery in sap system ?
entry.content:
We solved this problem by this way. Because the delivery is deleted from SAP and you wont be able view the deleted delivery order.

VL22 - Change report - no all information are there

MB51 - Material document list

- Reference field of the selection screen is your deleted delivery number - insert with * before and after for exampel 80144617. You will get info about material movements related to your delivery. When you double-click material document there detail about article number, batches, partner/customer etc.

 

The only place where you'll find the deleted delivery are the change tracking tables (CDHDR / CDPOS for header / detail respectively). Go to SE16 for CDHDR table and enter the deivery number (enclosed in asterisks '*') in OBJECTID field. With CDHDR fields you can also get the details from CDPOS.

 

Reward if you found this helpful

Regards,

Lalit

entry.title: Re: Bring SAP Concur Data to SAP Datasphere (Part-1)
entry.content:
Good one, Lalit

Do we have any direct integration possible without Integration suite.

entry.title: Re: What you need to get and stay certified

entry.title: Steps to Configure Deductible Factors in SuccessFactors Goal Template
entry.content:
Hi,

Our client has requested a custom configuration in the SuccessFactors Goal Template to incorporate deductible factors that influence the final rating on a scale of 1 to 5. Each selected deductible factor should reduce the final rating by 0.05, ensuring normalization of the final score. The template should support up to 10 deductible factors.

Could you confirm if this functionality is configurable in the Goal Template? If yes, we would appreciate detailed configuration steps to implement this solution effectively. Would appreciate your prompt response. 

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals  @Sam_Passman

@MridulSharmaSF

Thanks !!

Nidhi

entry.title: Re: How to use SAP Signavio to improve AI usage?
entry.content:
Hi Rogerio,
you can also check the offering of our partner KYP.ai. You can find more information about synergies and integrations with SAP Signavio in this blog post  and more information how to use KYP.ai to detect AI use case on their website. 
Feel free to get in touch with me for further details.

Best regards
Manuel

entry.title: Re: Ready to optimize your supply chain?
entry.content:
Hi @LMCP2024 
This looks like a new question.
I would like to encourage rather than adding a question to a blog post to always use our

SAP Learning Q&A. This is the perfect place to ask and answer questions about SAP Learning material and SAP Certificationsrneys. SAP Certification  Share your expertise or find the answers you need to become an expert.

Ask Questions section and post your question there.

You will find this Ask Questions section here:

https://jam2.sapjam.com/groups/63Iq2ZGd7B5F3pzlJgAxFW/overview_page/x9j04nslfNDbcayc3mDPe6

 

This makes it easier  to follow-up:

That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses

 

 

Would you mind to repost it ?

 

Kind regards

Margit

entry.title: How to insert a line item in the sap table ?
entry.content:
Dear Team.

In this blog I am going to show how we can insert a new line item in the sap table. please note this is not recommended but you should know 

Run SE16N and put the table name in which you want to add a line item.

& type /h to start debug

Then Type Variable name : GD-SAPEDIT  with X Value

 GD-EDIT        with X Value

After that press F8. After it you may add table data & delete the table data

 

Reward if you found helpfull.

Lalit Nagar

entry.title: Re: Error while setting build process workflow user task to "COMPLETED" using API Call
entry.content: Hello, you just have to make a task of approval, and take the id of your task, then you can approve this task with:

entry.title: Re: How to extract workflow details from S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello @Matheus,

I have a PR approval setup (item wise) in the system. I want to get a report out of the system which shows the setup.

For example:

PR approval Item wise setup:

For Purchase grp ABC -> We have 3 approvers.

For Purchase group XYZ -> We have 5 approvers. 

Is there a way to get the report of the setup of all purchase groups?

Regards

Rahul

entry.title: Different structure of WSDL in test and Production for integration in massage mapping
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

We have different WSDL structure for our integration in test and production. Difference is mostly related to the system IDs in test and production.

 

We have manually refreshed the WSDL in production system however in future it will be problematic i.e. whenever we make any changes in test and migrate to production ( download from test and upload into prod ) WSDL will get refreshed from the test.

 

And each time , we must update the WSDL structure in prod system manually which we don’t want to do.  

 

 Does anyone have idea how to manage migration of integration when WSDL is different between test and production ?

 

Thanks.

Sachin Yadav

entry.title: Re: How to set master recipe to do automatic goods receip...
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: How to unselect selected rows in list report table
entry.content:
Hi,

AFAIK there is no "Fiori Elements way" to deselect all table items, unfortunately. What you could do is breaking out of the Fiori Elements constraints and use direct control access to sap.m.Table. Please keep in mind that this violates the Fiori Elements Best Practices as this may break your app after a system upgrade.

Assuming you are using TypeScript and 'this' is of type sap.fe.templates.ListReport.ExtensionAPI, you could do the following:

 

import type Table from 'sap/m/Table';
import type XMLView from 'sap/ui/core/mvc/XMLView';

const appView = this._view as XMLView;
const appNamespace = appView.getId().split('::').at(0);
const smartTable = appView.byId(`${appNamespace}::RecordList--fe::table::Record::LineItem-innerTable`) as Table;
smartTable.removeSelections(true);

 

I tested this in a 1.120.20 environment (S/4HANA 2023).

Best regards,
Kevin

entry.title: Re: New AWS option for SAP ODP data extraction through AWS Glue
entry.content:
Hi @MarioDeFelipe 

thanks for sharing. I use the OData API of our SAP ByD a lot. So far, I used lambdas to extract data from SAP ByD for further processing with AWS services. The connector would simplify my work a lot, but when I tried to connect to our ByD Odata an invalid url error pops up. I provided the url I use so far (<tennant>.businessbydesign.cloud.sap), and for sap client# I used the one that is returned to mne in the headers of my usual OData queries.

Is there a way to get this working for SAP ByD OData?

Thanks and kind regards

entry.title: TOPIC - UI Data Protection Masking for SAP S/4HANA and its solution.
entry.content:
Hi Experts !!!

My component is UIDP 100 , SP05.

Requirement - I have a business partner table BUT000 which is masked based on certain conditions.  Now, it has around 2k entries which are masked. When i am trying to download this entries into an excel sheet taking 500 entries at a time, the masked value which was originally there in SE16N , after downloading to excel it is getting unmasked.

Please let me know is there any SNOTE i need to implement or any other solution,  i need to implement to make the values fully masked when we are downloading this in excel sheet .

 

Thanks,

PV

entry.title: sap.m.SplitApp linking within the JSON file
entry.content:
We have an application, a split app - sap.m.SplitApp

The data for the split app is held in a local data.json file.  Which some contains some HTML which has all been generated and showing as needed.

We have a need to link from the data in the JSON file to a page ID in the split app.

For Example -

In the data.json file we have

"page1Intro": "<p> If your looking for page 2 <Link text="Click Here" press="onPressNavToPage1"/> </p>"

Please note the " have been converted to "

in the View.xml

<Label text="Introduction" id="labelheaSTD003"/>
<FormattedText htmlText="{policyModel>/0/page1Intro}" width="100%" id="inputheaSTD002"/>

When viewing its showing

Introduction    If your looking for page 2 

The link is not working.

Also tried <a href=#page2/><strong>Page 2</strong></a> but the HREF will only link to a DIV ID not a page ID, is it possible to add a DIV ID to a Split App?

entry.title: Re: OPRM Table?
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: how to identify the highest number of rows and the la...
entry.content:
There are plenty of examples of SQL queries around the INTERNET to help achieve that, just GOOGLE it... 

Example: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/42830329/how-to-find-biggest-table-in-each-tablespace-in-oracle
